name: Web Search
description: Search the web via SerpAPI (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, news, images, shopping, YouTube). Primary search tool.

name: Web Fetch
description: Fetch and extract text content from a URL. Handles redirects, respects robots. SSRF-guarded.

name: Scrape URL
description: Scrape a single URL and return clean text. Chain: Firecrawl → SerpAPI → Apify → Scrapling (anti-bot) → Crawl4AI → requests. Best for JS-heavy pages and Facebook/Instagram/Cloudflare-protected sites.

name: SerpAPI Multi-Engine Search
description: Search any engine via SerpAPI: Google, Google Flights, Google News, Google Images, Google Shopping, Google Local, Google Jobs, YouTube, Bing, Amazon, Walmart, Yelp, Tripadvisor.

name: Run Apify Actor
description: Run any Apify actor for web scraping, data extraction, flight prices, hotel rates, e-commerce. Use apify_actor_schema first to get required input fields.

name: Apify Actor Schema
description: Get input schema and example for any Apify actor before running it.

name: Browser Navigate
description: Navigate a Playwright browser to a URL. Use before browser_click, browser_fill, browser_html, browser_screenshot.

name: Browser Click
description: Click an element on the current browser page by CSS selector or text.

name: Browser Fill Form
description: Fill a form field on the current browser page.

name: Browser Screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the current browser page. Returns PNG image.

name: Browser Get HTML
description: Get HTML source of the current browser page.

name: Send Telegram Message
description: Send a message to the owner via Telegram, or to any chat_id when owner names the recipient.

name: Send WhatsApp Message
description: Send a WhatsApp message to any phone number (E.164 format). Only to numbers the owner explicitly names.

name: Send Email (Gmail)
description: Send an email via the owner's Gmail account.

name: Read Emails (Gmail)
description: Search and read emails from the owner's Gmail inbox.

name: Run Shell Command
description: Execute any shell command on the A1 VM as the agent user. Use sudo_run for privileged operations.

name: Run Privileged Shell Command
description: Execute a shell command with sudo (passwordless). Use for apt install, service management, system config.

name: Spot FX Rate
description: Get current foreign exchange rate between two currencies via Frankfurter API.

name: FX Rate History
description: Get historical foreign exchange rates for a currency pair over a date range.

name: Crypto Price
description: Get current cryptocurrency price via CoinGecko (Coinbase fallback). Supports BTC, ETH, and 10,000+ tokens.

name: Store Memory
description: Persist a key-value pair to the agent's encrypted memory store. Survives restarts.

name: Retrieve Memory
description: Retrieve a value from the agent's encrypted memory store by key.

name: Create Scheduled Task
description: Schedule a recurring task with a cron expression. Results delivered to Telegram/WhatsApp.

name: List Scheduled Tasks
description: List all active scheduled tasks with their next run time and status.

name: Delete Scheduled Task
description: Delete a scheduled task by job ID.

name: Get Current Time
description: Return current local date and time on the A1 VM host in ISO 8601 format.

name: Get OpenRouter Balance
description: Check current OpenRouter API credit balance and monthly spend.

name: Switch AI Model
description: Change the active AI model on myagent (via OpenRouter). Accepts partial names like 'sonnet', 'flash', 'deepseek'.

name: Read Excel File
description: Read content from a .xlsx or .xlsm file on the A1 VM. Returns all sheets as structured data.

name: Write Excel File
description: Write or update data in a .xlsx file on the A1 VM.

name: Inject Keystrokes
description: Inject keyboard input into a running application on the A1 VM display via xdotool/ydotool.

name: Get Credential
description: Retrieve an API key, token, password, or credential from the agent's encrypted secure store. Call with key='OPENAI_API_KEY' to get a value, or omit key to list all available credential names. Use this instead of hunting for keys in config files.
